_comment: Curated SETS = bundles vetted to be quality AND compatible together. NOT a per-tool registry (Smithery/mcpm do that). See port-a-whip-spec.md v0.3.

sets.description: Token-lean coding stack: semantic code navigation (cuts grep/read tool-calls) + shell-output compression. Two complementary layers, no hook collision.

token_profile.method: measured = ccusage A/B (portaw bench --ab/--how) on the set's canonical workload, or command-level rtk-gain instrumentation. calculated = tiktoken (cl100k_base proxy) on the verbatim live MCP tool-def JSON the host loads (host tokenizers vary ~10-15%). vendor-claimed = from vendor docs, unverified. estimated = reasoned, not measured. neutral = 0 MCP tool-defs by construction (CLI/hook), idle schema cost provably zero. delta_pct = signed % tokens saved (+ = saved); idle_def_tokens = always-on tool-def cost on load-all hosts (0 on Claude Code, which lazy-loads).

claude-code.sample: rtk: rtk-gain 320 cmds mixed real workload, 2026-06-05 (MEASURED). codegraph: deterministic into-context proxy 2026-06-08 (tiktoken on tool-returns, port-a-whip self-index) — relationship-question class only.
claude-code.note: Set provenance = weakest link. rtk component MEASURED 26.3% mixed (per-cmd best-case far higher: diff 74.9%, ls 68-87%; read 7.8%/grep 10.6% dilute). codegraph: DETERMINISTIC proxy ran 2026-06-08 (bench/out/codeintel/, tiktoken cl100k on what enters context, n=1 small repo) — QUESTION-TYPE-DEPENDENT: callers+impact of get_set = 172 tok vs grep+read trace 6,622 (~97% LESS, codegraph crushes its home turf, grep can't even trace impact); BUT narrow 'show me this one file' = codegraph_explore over-returns 3 files 2,894 tok vs targeted Read of config.py 606 (~4.8x MORE). So codegraph wins BIG on relationship/impact/callers (use callers/impact tools), LOSES on show-one-symbol (use Read; explore=Read-equivalent). NO single blanket delta_pct — depends on question mix. Full session ccusage A/B still pending (bench/guides/04-05, needs fresh sessions). CC idle defs = 0 (codegraph lazy-loads). ROP 3-LANE RUN 2026-06-09 (grep vs codegraph vs semble, 3 real repos algorithm/backend/frontend, the 5-question workload): ccusage token-delta NOT isolable (the 'fresh' sessions were not single-workload-clean -> session totals contaminated, 0.7M-20M tok), so CAPABILITY/COMPLETENESS became the decisive axis (same outcome shape as web-research). RESULT: codegraph answered 5/5 FULLY every trial via callers/impact/explore (~15 calls/trial); semble answered only the SEARCH-shaped questions (Q1 entrypoint partial/full, Q4 config full, Q5 arch full) but Q2 callers = always PARTIAL and Q3 impact = NOT/PARTIAL because semble has NO callers/callees/impact tool (find_related returns semantic-similar code, not structural refs; also missed small/unindexed files + cross-repo config). One head-to-head trial: codegraph 5/5 FULLY in ~15 calls vs semble 1/5 FULLY 3/5 PARTIAL 1/5 NOT in 23 calls. CONFIRMS the host-conditional anchor on CAPABILITY grounds: codegraph earns CC (idle-free + the only one that answers refactor/impact/callers); semble's idle-leanness only pays off where the workload is pure search. VENDOR BENCHES (external, 2026-06-09, provenance vendor-claimed but CORROBORATED by paw's capability run + proxy): codegraph (colbymchenry, 7 repos×7 langs, MEDIAN of 4 runs/arm) = 59% fewer tokens + 70% fewer tool-calls + 35% cheaper + 49% faster, with include_source-whole-file = 64% WORSE (matches paw's show-one-file 4.8x-more proxy finding exactly); semble (MinishLab, 1250 pairs×63 repos) = 98% fewer tokens but SEARCH-RETRIEVAL only (94% recall@2k, NDCG 0.854 — silent on callers/impact = consistent with paw's capability gap). These two published benches + caveats independently agree with paw's findings, closing the clean-delta question without a paw-owned isolated-session run (which is still the only path to a paw `measured` label).

codex.sample: semble anchor on load-all host: tiktoken cl100k on live MCP schemas = 509 tok (search 202 + find_related 226 + instructions 81). codegraph alt = 1615.
codex.note: rtk (hook-proxy, 0 def) + semble (509 idle) = the load-all efficiency stack. codegraph (1615) reserved for CC where idle is free. rtk runtime saving measured 26.3% on CC host; semble runtime clean-delta still unmeasured (ROP run sessions contaminated), but its CAPABILITY ceiling is now confirmed: search-shaped Qs only, NO callers/callees/impact (ROP 2026-06-09). So on load-all hosts semble = the idle-lean DISCOVERY anchor; if refactor-impact/caller-audit is needed, swap semble->codegraph (idle 1615).

gemini.sample: semble anchor on load-all host: tiktoken cl100k on live MCP schemas = 509 tok (search 202 + find_related 226 + instructions 81). codegraph alt = 1615.
gemini.note: rtk (hook-proxy, 0 def) + semble (509 idle) = the load-all efficiency stack. codegraph (1615) reserved for CC where idle is free. rtk runtime saving measured 26.3% on CC host; semble runtime clean-delta still unmeasured (ROP run sessions contaminated), but its CAPABILITY ceiling is now confirmed: search-shaped Qs only, NO callers/callees/impact (ROP 2026-06-09). So on load-all hosts semble = the idle-lean DISCOVERY anchor; if refactor-impact/caller-audit is needed, swap semble->codegraph (idle 1615).

mcp.purpose: Pre-indexed local code knowledge graph. MCP tools codegraph_context (map a region) + codegraph_explore (symbol + relationships). Cuts token/tool-calls vs grep+read.
mcp._config_source_note: VERIFIED 2026-06-05 via `codegraph install --print-config <id>` (codegraph CLI v on PATH). Host id mapping: claude | codex | gemini | cursor (NOT 'claude-code'; full id set: claude,cursor,codex,opencode,hermes,gemini,antigravity,kiro). paw fetches the block then patches via its own patcher (uniform backup/rollback). DRIFT CORRECTED: earlier shape used `npx -y @colbymchenry/codegraph` — actual canonical command is the bare `codegraph` binary (installed on PATH) with `type:stdio`.

mcp_config_per_host._note: VERIFIED --print-config output 2026-06-05. claude/codex/gemini identical (serve --mcp); cursor adds workspace path.

setup_shim._why: MCP config alone leaves codegraph DEAD — it needs a built index. This is the residue patcher cannot do (vendor code). Scope to chosen project only.

mcp._self_installs_note: codegraph ships its own cross-host installer that patches ALL hosts interactively. paw does NOT delegate to it (no host-scoping, no uniform rollback, interactive). paw self-patches the single chosen host via config_source/mcp_config, then runs the index shim.
mcp._host_anchor_note: CC lazy-loads MCP defs -> idle ~0, so codegraph's richer toolset (callers/callees/impact/explore, 8 tools = 1615 tok CALCULATED cl100k) is free here and wins. On load-all hosts (Codex/Gemini) those 1615 tok are ALWAYS-ON -> use semble instead (see semble entry).

mcp.purpose: Semantic code search MCP (2 tools: search + find_related). Answers 'where/how' from an index instead of grep+read. Leaner than codegraph on load-all hosts: 509 tok idle (CALCULATED cl100k, 2 tools+instructions) vs codegraph 1615 (8 tools) = 68% less always-on def cost.
mcp._host_anchor_note: Load-all hosts (Codex/Gemini) pay every tool-def at startup -> semble's 509 < codegraph's 1615 matters. TRADE: semble = search+similarity only; NO callers/callees/impact. If a workflow needs refactor-impact, codegraph wins despite the idle cost. Runtime token-saving (grep+read replacement) claimed ~98%; clean A/B delta NOT isolable on the ROP run (sessions contaminated). CAPABILITY GAP CONFIRMED 2026-06-09 (ROP 3-repo run): semble fully answers search-shaped Qs (entrypoint/config/architecture) but CANNOT answer 'all callers' (Q2 always PARTIAL) or 'signature-change impact' (Q3 NOT/PARTIAL) -- no structural callers/callees/impact tool, find_related returns semantic neighbors not references, and it missed small/unindexed files + cross-repo config. So semble is a SEARCH primitive, not a call-graph tool: fine as the load-all idle-lean anchor for discovery, but swap to codegraph the moment refactor/impact/caller-audit is in scope.

mcp_config_per_host._note: VERIFIED from MinishLab/semble docs/installation.md 2026-06-06. Needs uv/uvx on PATH.

claude._target: ~/.claude.json mcpServers.semble (NOT default anchor on CC; codegraph preferred)

non_mcp.purpose: PreToolUse hook rewrites Bash commands to rtk equivalents, compresses shell output (cargo/pytest/go test/git/grep/find/ls/tsc/eslint/docker/kubectl) before it enters context. 60-90% on common dev commands (vendor).
non_mcp._build_test_coverage_note: 2026-06-12 audit (vs an 'extend rtk with output summarization' proposal): rtk ALREADY ships the build/test summarizer class — `rtk test` (failures-only), `rtk summary <cmd>` (generic heuristic), `rtk err <cmd>` (errors-only), `rtk pipe` (stdin filter), plus per-tool filters (pytest/cargo/tsc/jest/vitest/mypy/ruff/lint/next/dotnet/npm/playwright). Local proof: `rtk pytest` on a 297-test suite -> ONE line ('Pytest: 297 passed'). DO NOT propose building this. The REAL gap is ADOPTION: the hook does not rewrite interpreter-prefixed forms (`py -m pytest`, `python -m ...`) -> those runs enter context raw; call the rtk form explicitly. Mechanism note: a PostToolUse 'output interceptor' is impossible on Claude Code (output already entered context when the event fires) — PreToolUse command-rewrite is the only working model, i.e. rtk's existing one. Also `rtk discover` scanned 0 sessions on this Windows box (CC history path mismatch, upstream issue).

install.label: install rtk binary
install._alt: macOS/Linux can also: brew install rtk (if tapped). Windows winget id = rtk-ai.rtk (auto-runnable).

install.label: install hook + RTK.md into host
install._alt_manual: rtk init -g --no-patch  (prints hook for paw to apply)

hook_entry._note: VERIFIED 2026-06-05 from live host settings.json (rtk init writes this). paw can write it directly via its own patcher (rtk init -g --no-patch to skip rtk's own write).
hook_entry._per_host: command arg = target agent: `rtk hook claude` (Claude Code) | `rtk hook gemini` (Gemini). Cursor/aider per rtk --agent.

non_mcp._install_note: rtk is non-MCP (never in any MCP registry by definition) — exactly the gap paw fills. It self-installs its hook; paw orchestrates the step + records it for clean `portaw remove`. NO shell=True execution of community-supplied strings (RCE) — these install steps are curated/reviewed, pinned, and run as a fixed vendor script.

non_mcp.purpose: AST-pattern search/lint/rewrite (Rust, tree-sitter, polyglot). Fills the STRUCTURAL rung between lexical grep (host-native ripgrep) and graph (codegraph/semble): matches code SHAPE (`foo($$$ARGS)`) not text — no comment/string/whitespace false-positive lines entering context; and a multi-file codemod (`--rewrite`) = ONE bounded command + unified diff instead of N Edit round-trips. 0 MCP tool-defs.
non_mcp._axis_note: axis = ↓token, two structural mechanisms (provenance: CALCULATED — paw deterministic A/B 2026-06-12, tiktoken cl100k on real tool output, bench/_astgrep_ab.py, on the paw repo itself = a CLEAN/low-noise codebase so these are conservative): (1) precision — `route(` text-grep 242 tok (9 hit-lines incl. the `def`, substrings, any comment/string mention) vs ast-grep `route($$$A)` call-shape 74 tok = +69.4% (structural match returns only true call sites, tighter form); (2) bulk codemod (rename across files) — ast-grep `--rewrite` preview diff 291 tok vs a grep->read->edit loop 1,217 tok (tight +/-3 windows, LOWER bound) to 10,961 tok (full affected files, UPPER bound) = +76.1% to +97.3%. Completes the lexical/structural/graph triad inside ONE set; each rung answers a different question class (text? shape? relationships?) — no overlap with codegraph (no call-graph here) or rtk (different surface, see compat). Noisier real-world codebases (heavy comments/strings/substring collisions) widen mechanism-1 well past 69%.

install.label: install ast-grep (npm)
install._alt_pip: py -m pip install ast-grep-cli (this box) / pip install ast-grep-cli

non_mcp.usage_hint: ast-grep run -p 'console.log($$$A)' src/   |   rewrite: ast-grep run -p '$F.unwrap()' --rewrite '$F.expect("msg")' -U   |   rules: ast-grep scan (sgconfig.yml)

optional_skill._note: Skill in the ast-grep GitHub org (726★) teaching the agent to WRITE ast-grep rules (plugin-marketplace wrapper, not a separate tool). Optional — the CLI alone is the set entry; skill = 0 idle (progressive disclosure).

non_mcp._verified: 2026-06-12 web: ast-grep/ast-grep 14.5k★ MIT v0.43.0 (2026-05-25); install commands quoted from README (npm/pip/scoop/brew/cargo; no winget found — absence from README page, not proof). agent-skill repo lives in the ast-grep org. INSTALL-TESTED 2026-06-12 on this Windows box: `npm install --global @ast-grep/cli` -> ast-grep 0.43.0 on PATH, `portaw verify efficiency-starter` gate PASS (codegraph+rtk+ast-grep ok). Deterministic A/B RAN (bench/_astgrep_ab.py): precision +69.4%, codemod +76-97% (numbers in _axis_note). WINDOWS NOTE: npm ships a `.CMD`/`.ps1` shim that subprocess(shell=False) cannot launch directly — resolve the real `%APPDATA%\npm\node_modules\@ast-grep\cli\ast-grep.exe`. CAUTION: `--rewrite ... -U/--update-all` APPLIES in place (mutated repo files during the first A/B run; reverted); use `--rewrite` WITHOUT -U for a preview diff. rtk collision: NONE — rtk's PreToolUse rewrites only its known command list; `ast-grep` passes through, and its output is already bounded by the match (nothing for rtk to compress).

sets.compat_notes: CURATION VALUE — DIFFERENT LAYERS, no collision. (1) codegraph/semble = MCP context provider, adds tools, NO host hook. (2) rtk = PreToolUse hook, compresses Bash OUTPUT, adds NO MCP tools. (3) ast-grep = plain CLI, no hook, no MCP def; rtk passes it through (not in rtk's rewrite list) and its output is bounded by the match. Together the set covers the full search triad — lexical (host-native ripgrep) / STRUCTURAL (ast-grep) / GRAPH (codegraph callers/impact) — plus output compression (rtk); each rung answers a different question class, no redundancy. Install order: codegraph (config patch + index build) then rtk (hook) then ast-grep (any time). rtk supports claude-code/cursor/gemini/aider; codegraph supports claude-code/codex/gemini/cursor/opencode; ast-grep = any host with a shell — INTERSECTION for full-set = claude-code, gemini, cursor (codex gets codegraph+ast-grep; aider gets rtk+ast-grep).

optional_sources.note: Graph-search engine (resolved symbol edges, 'who calls this') claiming 3-50x token cuts vs grep workflows (vendor-claimed, zzet.org write-up 2026). RIVAL to the codegraph anchor, NOT additive — never load both. Held out of the set: (1) needs a resident DAEMON (friction vs paw's no-daemon lean), (2) no paw bench vs codegraph's measured proxy + ROP capability run. Re-evaluate only via a head-to-head A/B (DOGFOOD-PENDING class).

Already vetted 2026-06-09 (see verified.headroom_challenge): complementary layer (+12% additive over rtk's 88% in a real dual-deploy), kept OPTIONAL because PyPI ships no Windows wheel (Rust/maturin build friction). Promoted here from the verified-notes so the rung is visible to the router.

rejected.reason: Experimental MCP wrapping the LOCAL ast-grep CLI = always-on idle defs for zero capability delta — the CLI is already one bounded shell call. Same lean-vs-heavy verdict as duckdb-MCP (data-query) and playwright-MCP (browser-automation).
rejected.axis: idle-def blow for no capability delta

verified.refs_confirmed: 2026-06-05 — codegraph npm @colbymchenry/codegraph confirmed; rtk github.com/rtk-ai/rtk MIT confirmed. 2026-06-12 — ast-grep/ast-grep 14.5k★ MIT v0.43.0 confirmed via web + INSTALL-TESTED on this box (npm global, ast-grep 0.43.0, verify gate PASS). Structural-rung delta = CALCULATED via paw deterministic A/B (bench/_astgrep_ab.py): precision +69.4%, codemod +76-97% (conservative — measured on the clean paw repo).
verified.config_block_confirmed: 2026-06-05 — codegraph mcp_config captured from `codegraph install --print-config <id>` (claude/codex/gemini/cursor, drift corrected npx→bare binary); rtk hook entry captured from live settings.json (PreToolUse/Bash/`rtk hook claude`). Both stored in mcp_config_per_host + hook_entry.
verified.token_profile_benchmarked: partial — rtk anchor LOCKED 2026-06-05 (rtk-gain: 26.3% mixed / 35.6K saved / 320 cmds, command-level paired A/B, zero cache noise). codegraph half pending Phase-2.
verified.rtk_anchor_decision: LOCKED. rtk net-positive on real mixed workload (26.3%); strongest on bulk/structured output (diff/ls/build). Beats peer-anchor bar — rivals (context-mode/lean-ctx/snip/ecotokens) do NOT self-report a reproducible command-level number, rtk does. No swap.
verified.rtk_local_bench_2026_06_09: DETERMINISTIC corroboration (paw's own, bench/_compress_ab.py: tiktoken cl100k on raw-vs-rtk command output, rop-backend git-heavy workload, zero ccusage/cache noise): git_diff 69.1%, git_log_stat 53.5%, git_status 70.6% -> TOTAL 63.5% (raw 14,643 -> rtk 5,340). Confirms rtk's per-command bulk/structured strength independently of vendor numbers; the 26.3% mixed figure is lower only because read/grep dilute a mixed session.
verified.headroom_challenge_2026_06_09: RESOLVED — NOT a rival/swap. rtk (PreToolUse hook, command-OUTPUT) and headroom (API-proxy, session/history/RAG) are COMPLEMENTARY LAYERS that STACK. Real 1-month dual-deploy (andrewpatterson.dev): rtk = 88% of savings, headroom = +12% ADDITIVE. headroom per-model 32-59% (headline 60-95% = per-content best-case). rtk stays the anchor (bigger lever + standalone-EXE 0-build on Windows); headroom = CANDIDATE OPTIONAL additive rung but Windows install friction is real (PyPI ships NO Windows wheel — macOS+manylinux only; Rust/maturin build fails without a toolchain on py3.12 AND 3.14). Keep headroom optional, not core. Detail: deep-vet §6 challenge B.

code_intel_anchor_by_host._decision: 2026-06-06 host-conditional anchor (idle-def calculated, runtime A/B pending).
code_intel_anchor_by_host.claude-code: codegraph (idle free on lazy-load host; richer: callers/callees/impact)
code_intel_anchor_by_host.codex: semble (509 vs codegraph 1615 idle tok = 68% leaner on load-all host; search-only)
code_intel_anchor_by_host._n1: Only ONE code-intel MCP per host (codegraph XOR semble) + rtk (0 def) = 1 active MCP server/host, under N1 ceiling. No redundancy: the set never loads both on one host.

sets.description: Permissions + supply-chain guardrails for autonomous coding agents. Covers the harness component codegraph/rtk leave empty: a deterministic action-guard, secret leak prevention, and dependency-vuln defense. Entirely CLI/hook — ZERO MCP tool-defs, so it adds no per-session schema overhead on any host.
sets._criterion_note: DIFFERENT AXIS from efficiency-starter. paw's core gate is 'reduce tokens OR improve context quality'; this set's PRIMARY value is the harness Permissions component (prevent catastrophic actions, block secret commits, catch malicious deps) — the 5th harness component (Tools/Knowledge/Observation/Action/Permissions). Secondary token benefit: every tool emits STRUCTURED/BOUNDED findings (JSON file:line:rule) instead of raw dumps the agent must scroll. Curated here because Permissions is the gap no registry/host covers; not over-claimed as a token-saver.

token_profile.method: measured = ccusage A/B (portaw bench --ab/--how) on the set's canonical workload, or command-level rtk-gain instrumentation. calculated = tiktoken (cl100k_base proxy) on the verbatim live MCP tool-def JSON the host loads (host tokenizers vary ~10-15%). vendor-claimed = from vendor docs, unverified. estimated = reasoned, not measured. neutral = 0 MCP tool-defs by construction (CLI/hook), idle schema cost provably zero. delta_pct = signed % tokens saved (+ = saved); idle_def_tokens = always-on tool-def cost on load-all hosts (0 on Claude Code, which lazy-loads).

claude-code.sample: 0 MCP servers in set (nah=PreToolUse hook, gitleaks/osv-scanner/infisical=CLI).
claude-code.note: Token cost provably zero: no MCP tool-defs loaded on any host. Secondary tok-saving where bounded JSON replaces raw scan dumps (unmeasured, upside).

codex.note: CLI/hook tools invoked on demand; no startup schema cost even on load-all host.

non_mcp.purpose: Deterministic PreToolUse classifier that distinguishes safe vs dangerous shell actions (e.g. `rm dist/` vs `rm ~/.bashrc`, `curl evil | bash`). No LLM in the path = no latency. Project override is TIGHTEN-ONLY (supply-chain-safe: a repo cannot LOOSEN your guard).

install.label: wire guard into Claude Code
install._alt_plugin_only: claude plugin marketplace add manuelschipper/nah@claude-marketplace --scope user && claude plugin install nah@nah --scope user

non_mcp._note: WARNING surfaced by nah: do NOT pair with `--dangerously-skip-permissions` / `--enable-auto-mode` — those bypass the guard entirely. Codex/Gemini guard = roadmap (nah is CC-first today).

non_mcp.purpose: Fast (Go) regex+entropy secret scanner. Agent pipes staged content through `gitleaks ... stdin` before commit, or scans the repo. Structured JSON/SARIF output (file:line:rule:secret) = bounded, not a raw grep dump.

install.label: install gitleaks
install._alt_linux: no apt/dnf pkg; brew (Linuxbrew) or the docker image / GitHub release binary

non_mcp.usage_hint: cat <file> | gitleaks -v stdin   |   gitleaks detect --report-format json --report-path -

non_mcp.purpose: Google's multi-language dependency vulnerability scanner (npm/PyPI/cargo/Go/…) against the OSV database. Catches a known-bad dep BEFORE the agent runs `npm i`/`pip install`. Repo ships AGENTS.md + llms.txt = explicitly agent-aware. Structured advisory JSON.

install.label: install osv-scanner
install._note_macos: macOS uses brew (formula osv-scanner, bottle = no Go toolchain needed). CI 2026-06-13: go is NOT preinstalled on macOS runners — `go install` errors 'No such file or directory: go'. Most mac devs have brew, not Go.
install._note_path_go: On windows/linux the go-install path drops the binary in $(go env GOPATH)/bin (~/go/bin), NOT on PATH by default — install succeeds but `osv-scanner` is not found until that dir is on PATH (standard go setup). Also requires a Go toolchain. _alt_brew (Linux: needs Linuxbrew) / _alt_binary avoid both.

non_mcp.purpose: Injects secrets as environment variables at runtime via `infisical run -- <cmd>`. The agent calls APIs/tools that need credentials WITHOUT the raw secret ever entering the prompt/context. Directly serves the Permissions principle: least-exposure of secrets.

install.label: install infisical
install._note_linux: brew tap infisical/get-cli is macOS-ONLY (CI 2026-06-13: 'macOS is required for this software' on Linux). Linux uses the official cross-platform npm package @infisical/cli (argv-safe, no root). apt/rpm repos exist but need curl|sudo (shell+root -> would be print-only).
install._alt_apt: curl -1sLf 'https://artifacts-cli.infisical.com/setup.deb.sh' | sudo -E bash && sudo apt-get install -y infisical
install._alt_scoop: scoop bucket add org https://github.com/Infisical/scoop-infisical.git ; scoop install infisical

sets.compat_notes: CURATION VALUE — four NON-overlapping guard layers, all CLI/hook (0 MCP def): (1) nah = action guard (PreToolUse, blocks dangerous shell), (2) gitleaks = secret EGRESS guard (don't commit keys), (3) osv-scanner = dependency INGRESS guard (don't install malware), (4) infisical = secret EXPOSURE guard (don't put raw keys in context). No collision: only nah touches PreToolUse; the three CLIs are invoked on demand and add no host hook. OPTIONAL hardening (not in core set, heavier): microsandbox (libkrun microVM, hardware isolation) for true sandboxed exec; ggshield if you need ACTIVE secret validation (500+ vendor APIs) beyond gitleaks detection. EXCLUSIVE: gitleaks ⊕ detect-secrets (pick one detector; gitleaks = speed, detect-secrets = baseline-accept-existing workflow). Full-set host = claude-code (nah is CC-only today); codex/gemini/aider get the 3 CLIs (gitleaks/osv/infisical) without the action-guard until nah expands.

verified.status: verified — install-tested end-to-end (bench/guides/10-installs.md). All 4 tools on PATH, gate PASS.
verified.refs_confirmed: 2026-06-05 — gh + README verified: nah 449★ v0.9.0 MIT; gitleaks 27.5k★ v8.30.1 MIT; osv-scanner 10.4k★ v2.3.8 Apache-2.0; infisical 27.2k★ v0.160.10. Install commands quoted from official READMEs/docs.
verified.config_block_confirmed: nah wired (9 matchers × 3 hook events) into ~/.claude/settings.json; gitleaks/osv-scanner/infisical = CLI on PATH.

sets.description: Anti-hallucination knowledge layer: live, version-correct library docs injected on demand. Stops the agent inventing APIs / using stale method signatures. Single lean MCP (2 tool-defs) — does NOT touch local code (complements codegraph: external docs vs in-repo graph).
sets._criterion_note: Axis = IMPROVE CONTEXT QUALITY (the second half of paw's core gate). Context7 is widely cited as the highest-impact MCP for code-quality because hallucinated/outdated API usage is a top failure mode. Token angle: it FETCHES only the needed doc slice on demand (progressive disclosure) rather than the agent guessing then debugging — fewer wasted correction loops. 2 tool-defs = N1-safe.

token_profile.method: measured = ccusage A/B (portaw bench --ab/--how) on the set's canonical workload, or command-level rtk-gain instrumentation. calculated = tiktoken (cl100k_base proxy) on the verbatim live MCP tool-def JSON the host loads (host tokenizers vary ~10-15%). vendor-claimed = from vendor docs, unverified. estimated = reasoned, not measured. neutral = 0 MCP tool-defs by construction (CLI/hook), idle schema cost provably zero. delta_pct = signed % tokens saved (+ = saved); idle_def_tokens = always-on tool-def cost on load-all hosts (0 on Claude Code, which lazy-loads).

claude-code.sample: 2 Context7 defs lazy-loaded on CC -> idle ~0; full cost (927 tok) only when surfaced.
claude-code.note: On-demand doc fetch replaces guess-then-fix loops (quality-primary). See codex/gemini for the load-all idle cost.

codex.sample: tiktoken cl100k_base on verbatim live def JSON 2026-06-06: resolve-library-id 611 + query-docs 316 = 927 tok.
codex.note: ALWAYS-ON cost on load-all host (defs loaded at startup). Host tokenizer (GPT o200k) differs from cl100k proxy ~10-15%. Net acceptable: 927 tok buys anti-hallucination doc grounding (2 tools, under N1 ceiling).

gemini.sample: same def JSON, cl100k proxy = 927 tok; Gemini tokenizer differs.
gemini.note: ALWAYS-ON on load-all host; same trade as codex.

mcp.purpose: Two tools: resolve-library-id (name → Context7 id) + query-docs (id → current docs). Pulls version-correct docs for the exact package in use, killing API hallucination. Local stdio via npx; remote endpoint https://mcp.context7.com/mcp also available.
mcp._config_source_note: mcp_config below = canonical local-stdio shape from official README (npm @upstash/context7-mcp). Verify against client-specific setup page before relying on it.

auth._note: API key OPTIONAL — free key at context7.com/dashboard raises rate limits. If used, inject via env CONTEXT7_API_KEY as a ${VAR} ref, NEVER plaintext in config (spec §security). env stays {} when keyless.

sets.compat_notes: CURATION VALUE — pairs cleanly with efficiency-starter: Context7 = EXTERNAL library knowledge (docs on the internet), codegraph = INTERNAL code knowledge (your repo's graph). No overlap, no host-surface collision (Context7 adds 2 MCP tools, no hook). Stacking note (N1): if a user installs efficiency-starter + context-quality together on a LOAD-ALL host (Codex/Gemini), total active MCP servers = 2 (codegraph + context7) = within the ≤2-3 ceiling; adding a 3rd MCP server later needs a ceiling check. NOT bundled with GitHub MCP (87 tools = blows budget) — if code-search is wanted, use a thin search_code-only wrapper (backlog build-op #4), not the full GitHub MCP.

sets.description: For anyone shipping UI who wants to kill 'AI slop' and keep design fidelity. Catches the visual tells of machine-made frontends (purple gradients, bounce easing, cramped padding, dark glows, skipped headings, tiny touch targets) BEFORE they ship, and pulls real design tokens from Figma into code. Saves tokens by replacing guess-restyle-reguess loops with one deterministic audit pass.
sets._axis_note: axis = ↑quality (NOT token-primary). 0 MCP tool-defs → N1-free, 0 idle on every host. Stacks freely with efficiency-starter/secure-agent.

token_profile.method: measured = ccusage A/B (portaw bench --ab/--how) on the set's canonical workload, or command-level rtk-gain instrumentation. calculated = tiktoken (cl100k_base proxy) on the verbatim live MCP tool-def JSON the host loads (host tokenizers vary ~10-15%). vendor-claimed = from vendor docs, unverified. estimated = reasoned, not measured. neutral = 0 MCP tool-defs by construction (CLI/hook), idle schema cost provably zero. delta_pct = signed % tokens saved (+ = saved); idle_def_tokens = always-on tool-def cost on load-all hosts (0 on Claude Code, which lazy-loads).

claude-code.note: Quality-primary. Secondary ↓token: /impeccable audit catches slop in ONE deterministic pass vs many restyle-reguess round-trips (unmeasured, upside). 0 MCP idle.

codex.note: 0 MCP defs even on load-all host; skill/CLI invoked on demand.

non_mcp.purpose: Anti-AI-slop design audit. 27 deterministic anti-pattern rules + 12-rule LLM critique pass. Slash: /impeccable audit|polish|critique. CLI: npx impeccable detect src/. No API key for the deterministic rules.

install.label: install impeccable skill into the harness (.claude/ etc.)

non_mcp._verified: README pbakaus/impeccable 2026-06-06: skill-based (non-MCP), cross-harness, /impeccable + npx detect. anti-slop = 24 tells across AI-slop + general design quality.

non_mcp.purpose: Extract design tokens from Figma → CSS/SCSS/Tailwind/SwiftUI/Android/JSON. No Figma plugin / Dev Mode needed. Keeps implemented UI faithful to the design source (fidelity = quality axis).
non_mcp._secret_note: Needs a Figma personal access token in .env as FIGMA_TOKEN (or --token). NEVER hardcode — env/.env only (paw security rule). Optional only if the user works from Figma.
non_mcp._verified: README tortastudios/figtree-cli 2026-06-06: plain CLI (non-MCP), npm -g, FIGMA_TOKEN env, multi-format output.

optional_sources.note: OSS Claude-Design-alt PLATFORM (agent-agnostic SKILL.md/DESIGN.md, 259 skills, 142 design systems). Too heavy to bundle whole; treat as a SKILLS SOURCE — curate specific DESIGN.md systems into this set later. Apache-2.0.

sets.compat_notes: All tools non-MCP (skill + CLI) → 0 MCP tool-defs, 0 host-surface collision. N1-free: stacks with efficiency-starter + secure-agent on a load-all host without touching the ≤2-3 MCP ceiling. impeccable = the anchor (no key, deterministic); figtree-cli = optional, only for Figma-driven teams (needs FIGMA_TOKEN). open-design = a skills SOURCE to mine, not bundled.

verified.status: verified — install-tested + SLOP-CATCH EXERCISED 2026-06-15. impeccable v2.3.2 installed (npx impeccable skills install, detect CLI functional). Slop-catch now PROVEN on a real sloppy frontend (bench/_slopcatch/: a deliberately AI-slop page — purple gradient + dark glow, cramped padding, bounce/elastic easing, skipped h1->h3, tiny touch targets): `npx impeccable detect bench/_slopcatch/` flagged 5 anti-patterns = low-contrast x3 (4.0:1 / 2.8:1 / 1.2:1 vs WCAG AA 4.5:1), skipped-heading (h1 'Welcome' -> h3, missing h2), bounce-easing (cubic-bezier(0.68,-0.55,0.265,1.55) -> use ease-out-expo). Confirms the deterministic detector catches genuine slop, not just runs. NOTE: the CLI's deterministic rules fired on contrast/heading/easing; gradient/padding/touch-target tells are in the heavier /impeccable critique (LLM) pass, not the offline CLI rules — both shapes documented honestly. figtree-cli: optional, untested (needs FIGMA_TOKEN). open-design: deferred (skills source, not bundled).
verified.impeccable: verified (v2.3.2, npx impeccable skills install; detect CLI fires 5 anti-patterns on bench/_slopcatch/ slop fixture 2026-06-15)

sets.description: Token-lean web research: fetch + EXTRACT clean content (markdown, not raw-HTML dumps) and composable search, so the agent does its own research cheaply. Curates research PRIMITIVES the agent composes (search -> fetch -> extract, sources auditable), NOT an opaque answer-product connector that hides sourcing.
sets._axis_note: axis = BOTH halves of paw's gate. (1) DOWN-token: TARGETED extract (css-selector/semantic rank) returns only the relevant slice instead of a whole page the agent must scroll. (2) UP-quality: agent composes + can audit every source (no black-box synthesis). THE LINE (principled, not inherited from a drop-list): paw curates research PRIMITIVES (search/fetch/extract you compose and verify) and REJECTS answer-product connectors (perplexity-class) that synthesize the answer with opaque sourcing — those replace the agent's reasoning and defeat the same anti-hallucination axis as context-quality. This is why exa (composable search primitive) is allowed-but-optional while perplexity stays out, even though both are paid SaaS APIs: the split is primitive-vs-answer-product, not paid-vs-free. REDUNDANCY FLAG (advisor 2026-06-07): the plain Fetch anchor is NOT a token win where the HOST already ships a digest-fetch — Claude Code's native WebFetch already does URL->markdown->small-model-digest, so on CC the set's real value is the ADDITIVE rungs (Scrapling targeted/stealth extract, firecrawl JS-render, exa semantic), not Fetch. Fetch earns its keyless-anchor slot mainly on hosts WITHOUT a native digest-fetch (load-all hosts), where its 259-tok idle buys a capability CC gets for free. The token lever is targeted-extract-vs-full-page, NOT clean-vs-raw (no host emits raw-HTML dumps).

token_profile.method: measured = ccusage A/B (portaw bench --ab/--how) on the set's canonical workload, or command-level rtk-gain instrumentation. calculated = tiktoken (cl100k_base proxy) on the verbatim live MCP tool-def JSON the host loads (host tokenizers vary ~10-15%). vendor-claimed = from vendor docs, unverified. estimated = reasoned, not measured. neutral = 0 MCP tool-defs by construction (CLI/hook), idle schema cost provably zero. delta_pct = signed % tokens saved (+ = saved); idle_def_tokens = always-on tool-def cost on load-all hosts (0 on Claude Code, which lazy-loads).

claude-code.sample: 3-lane A/B RAN 2026-06-08 (deterministic tiktoken cl100k on saved tool-returns, bench/_count_tokens.py; 3 fixed URLs: PEP8 + json docs + click README; n=1, content-stable). COMPLETE lanes only: native 16,182 tok vs Scrapling-broad 21,025 vs Scrapling-TIGHT 2,599. Fetch MCP = 2,858 but DISQUALIFIED (max_length=5000 TRUNCATED 2/3 pages → failed completeness: PEP8 line-length+binary-op cut, json params cut mid-list).
claude-code.note: delta_pct=null DELIBERATELY: no GENERAL one-shot token win on CC. Findings: (1) native WebFetch beats plain Fetch (truncates→incomplete) AND broad Scrapling (whole-section selector 21,025 > native 16,182). → Fetch DEMOTED on CC (keep as load-all-only anchor). (2) Scrapling with TIGHT oracle selectors = 2,599 tok = ~84% < native, STILL COMPLETE = the targeted-extract lever is REAL at the CEILING. BUT ceiling hides DISCOVERY COST: selectors were picked knowing where answers live; a real agent must fetch the page first to find the selector (≈ one native fetch) → amortized one-shot cost > native. So the CC value = targeted extract ONLY for (a) repeated/known-structure pages, (b) JS-render pages native can't read — a CAPABILITY rung, not a general saver. idle 0 on CC (Fetch lazy-loads). Full run: registry/deep-vet.md web-research section; artifacts bench/out/.

codex.sample: tiktoken cl100k_base on verbatim live Fetch def JSON 2026-06-07 (modelcontextprotocol/servers list_tools): name+description+inputSchema = 259 tok (1059 chars). bench/_fetch_def_count.py.
codex.note: Load-all host pays this 259 at startup. LEANEST MCP anchor in any paw set (1 tool; cf. semble 509, Context7 927, codegraph 1615). Host-conditional like efficiency-starter: keep this no-key anchor on load-all; add at most ONE keyed extra (exa/firecrawl) at a time. delta_pct (clean-md vs raw-HTML) still PENDING A/B.

gemini.sample: same Fetch def JSON, cl100k proxy = 259 tok; Gemini tokenizer differs ~10-15%.
gemini.note: ALWAYS-ON 259 on load-all host; same lean trade as codex. delta_pct PENDING A/B.

mcp.purpose: Official reference MCP, ONE tool (`fetch`): pulls a URL and returns it as clean markdown instead of raw HTML. The lean, no-key extraction anchor — the agent reads only the readable content, not boilerplate/script/style noise. This is the on-thesis core: token cut by construction.
mcp._config_source_note: mcp-server-fetch is a reference STDIO server — it ships NO `--print-config` (unlike codegraph). So there is no vendor block to fetch; the canonical `uvx mcp-server-fetch` shape from the reference-servers README is authoritative, and mcp_config_per_host below was RENDERED by paw's own patcher (build_entry -> merge_json/merge_toml) and round-trip-validated 2026-06-15 (bench/_webresearch_fetch_config_capture.py).

mcp_config_per_host._note: RENDERED + round-trip-validated by paw's patcher 2026-06-15 (no vendor --print-config exists for a reference stdio server). Single canonical block patches cleanly into all three host formats; only file path + format differ.

auth._note: No API key. Optional: --ignore-robots-txt / custom user-agent per README.

mcp._verified: 2026-06-07 — def CAPTURED verbatim from modelcontextprotocol/servers list_tools(): 1 tool `fetch` (url required; max_length/start_index/raw optional). tiktoken cl100k = 259 tok (bench/_fetch_def_count.py). `verify` PASS (uvx on PATH). 2026-06-15 — per-host config CLOSED: mcp-server-fetch has no `--print-config`, so mcp_config_per_host was rendered through paw's real patcher merge funcs (CC/Gemini JSON mcpServers, Codex TOML [mcp_servers.fetch]) and re-parsed clean (bench/_webresearch_fetch_config_capture.py). config_block_confirmed.

optional_sources.note: FIRST rung — keyless adaptive extractor. CLI survives layout changes + pulls TARGETED content via css-selector (cut tokens vs full page), and as a CLI it is 0 idle def on load-all hosts. PREFER THE CLI: the built-in MCP exposes 10 tools (get/bulk_get/fetch/bulk_fetch/stealthy_fetch/bulk_stealthy_fetch/screenshot/open_session/close_session/list_sessions) = NOT lean. CALCULATED 2026-06-15 (bench/_webresearch_scrapling_def_count.py, tiktoken cl100k on the REAL FastMCP tool schemas built in-process from scrapling 0.4.9) = 15,859 tok idle on a load-all host (per-tool: bulk_stealthy_fetch 2258 / stealthy_fetch 2168 / bulk_get 2019 / bulk_fetch 2003 / open_session 1959 / get 1936 / fetch 1915 / screenshot 1097 / list_sessions 298 / close_session 195) — the browser fetchers each carry ~30 optional params. That is ~10x codegraph (1615) and ~17x the Fetch anchor (259): the MCP form is N1-CATASTROPHIC on load-all hosts. So on load-all hosts use the CLI (0 idle); MCP form reserved for CC (lazy) if you want sessions/screenshot.
optional_sources.cli_usage: scrapling extract get 'https://example.com' out.md   |   scrapling extract fetch 'URL' out.md --css-selector '#main'   |   scrapling extract stealthy-fetch 'URL' out.html
optional_sources.axis: down-token (targeted extract; CLI = 0 idle def on load-all)
optional_sources._verified: 2026-06-07 — CLI commands + install from README; MCP config `scrapling mcp` + 10-tool list from scrapling.readthedocs.io/ai/mcp-server. 2026-06-15 — MCP def-token count NO LONGER an estimate: scrapling 0.4.9 installed keyless on this box, so the REAL FastMCP tool schemas were built in-process (bench/_webresearch_scrapling_def_count.py mirrors ScraplingMCPServer.serve() without server.run(), then await server.list_tools() + tiktoken cl100k) = 15,859 tok / 10 tools (provenance CALCULATED-from-source, not WebFetch paraphrase). Confirms MCP form is heavy (~10x codegraph); CLI form fully VERIFIED + the lean default.

optional_sources.note: Keyless SEARCH primitive: self-hosted SearXNG metasearch. The on-thesis search rung when you won't use a SaaS key. Cost = you run a SearXNG instance and enable JSON format in its settings.yml. 2 tools (searxng_web_search + web_url_read) = lean (idle est. ~semble-class 2-tool ≈ 400-550 tok; exact count needs live dump). Strong candidate to promote to default-on alongside Fetch IF the keyless-search need is common.
optional_sources.axis: down-token + up-quality (ranked results, no key, composable, auditable)
optional_sources._verified: 2026-06-07 — config from ihor-sokoliuk/mcp-searxng README (npx -y mcp-searxng, env SEARXNG_URL); 2 tools (searxng_web_search + web_url_read) confirmed from src. Requires a running SearXNG with JSON format enabled. Exact def-token count needs a live schema dump (deferred — needs an instance; WebFetch paraphrases descriptions, can't yield a clean calculated count).

optional_sources.note: Keyed semantic-search PRIMITIVE: web_search_exa + web_fetch_exa (web_search_advanced_exa disabled by default). Returns ranked results + contents the agent composes. ON the primitive side of the line (composable, auditable sources) — this is the principled reason it is IN while perplexity (answer-product) is OUT, NOT because of price (both are paid SaaS). OPT-IN: needs EXA_API_KEY.
optional_sources.axis: down-token + up-quality (semantic rank cuts junk; composable, auditable sources)
optional_sources._verified: 2026-06-07 — config + tools + env from exa-labs/exa-mcp-server README (npx -y exa-mcp-server, EXA_API_KEY). Def-token count PENDING (3 tools, 1 disabled).

optional_sources.note: LAST rung — only for JS/SPA pages Fetch + Scrapling can't render. CORRECTION 2026-06-07: the firecrawl MCP has NO scrape-only subset switch — it loads ALL ~12 tools (scrape/batch/map/search/crawl/extract/agent/monitor...) = N1-BLOWING, so the lean form is NOT the MCP. For lean use call the REST scrape endpoint directly (curl https://api.firecrawl.dev/v1/scrape -> clean md, 0 idle def). Reserve the full MCP for CC-only (lazy) if you truly need crawl/map. Key-required.
optional_sources.axis: down-token via REST scrape (lean); MCP form is heavy/no-subset (CC-only if needed)
optional_sources._verified: 2026-06-07 — MCP config + full tool list (~12) from firecrawl/firecrawl-mcp-server README. No tool-subset switch FOUND IN THE README (absence-of-evidence from one page, NOT proof firecrawl lacks it). Regardless, the safe lean path is the REST scrape endpoint, not the full MCP; the earlier 'lean MCP subset' wording was unsupported.

rejected.reason: OFF-THESIS by the primitive-vs-answer-product line: these synthesize the answer with opaque sourcing (perplexity) or are SaaS workspace connectors (notion/slack) = general-installer / Smithery territory, not a token-or-quality lever the agent composes. Holding this line IS paw's identity vs a generic MCP installer.

47-tool MCP = N1-blowing; browser DRIVING is a capability, not a research-token lever. The lean browser need (read page text, snapshot) belongs in a future browser-automation set anchored on browser-harness (0 idle def), NOT here.

sets.compat_notes: CURATION VALUE — a keyless LEAN core + a flagged ladder. Default-on = Fetch MCP only (1 tool, no key, HTML->md, 259 tok CALCULATED idle on load-all / 0 on CC) so the set is N1-trivial and certain. optional_sources = an explicit ladder, keyless rungs FIRST: Scrapling CLI (keyless adaptive extract, 0 idle) -> SearXNG (keyless self-host search) -> exa (keyed semantic search) -> firecrawl (keyed, JS-heavy ONLY; lean path = REST scrape endpoint, NOT the ~12-tool MCP which has no subset switch). N1 on load-all hosts: keep the keyless anchor active; add at most ONE keyed extra at a time. Pairs cleanly with context-quality (Context7 = curated lib docs; web-research = open web) and efficiency-starter (in-repo graph) — three non-overlapping knowledge surfaces. Formalizes the firecrawl+exa research pattern the ecc deep-research skill already uses, curated onto paw's token/quality axis.

verified.status: DRAFT (CC A/B DONE 2026-06-08). thesis line locked (primitive vs answer-product). Fetch anchor def CAPTURED + tiktoken'd (259 tok, calculated idle). optional configs VERIFIED from READMEs: Scrapling CLI, searxng-mcp, exa-mcp. **CC 3-lane A/B RAN** (deterministic file-tokenize, n=1 content-stable): native 16,182 beats Fetch (truncated→incomplete, DEMOTED on CC) and broad-Scrapling (21,025); TIGHT-selector Scrapling 2,599 (~84% < native, complete) = ceiling lever real BUT hides discovery cost → CC = capability rung, no general delta_pct. STILL PENDING before full 'verified': (1) ~~per-host config for Fetch~~ DONE 2026-06-15 — rendered by paw's patcher + round-trip-validated (no vendor --print-config for a reference stdio server); (2) def-token counts for optional MCPs — Scrapling DONE (15,859 tok, the only keyless one); exa/firecrawl/searxng still need keys/instances; (3) load-all-host value confirmation (Fetch's 259-idle buys capability Codex/Gemini lack natively — calculated, not A/B'd; needs a live load-all host).
verified.config_block_confirmed: Fetch CLOSED 2026-06-15 — no vendor --print-config exists for a reference stdio server, so mcp_config_per_host was rendered by paw's own patcher (merge_json/merge_toml) + round-trip-validated for CC/Gemini JSON + Codex TOML (bench/_webresearch_fetch_config_capture.py). optional MCP config shapes verified from READMEs.
verified.token_profile_benchmarked: partial — CC MEASURED 2026-06-08 (3-lane, deterministic tiktoken, bench/web-research-workload.md + bench/guides/01-03). load-all hosts = calculated idle only.
verified.idle_calculated: Fetch 259 tok cl100k (load-all); 0 on CC. Scrapling MCP = 15,859 tok / 10 tools (CALCULATED-from-source 2026-06-15, bench/_webresearch_scrapling_def_count.py — confirms the CLI-not-MCP curation; MCP form is N1-catastrophic). exa/firecrawl/searxng MCP defs STILL not tokenized (need a key/instance for a live schema dump — Scrapling was the only keyless one).

sets.description: Lean browser-DRIVING capability for agents that must actually use a real browser (fill forms, upload files, navigate JS/SPA flows, scrape pages JS-render breaks). A thin self-healing CDP harness driven as a skill — ZERO MCP tool-defs — instead of the heavy playwright-MCP (47 tools) that blows the N1 ceiling for the same capability.
sets._axis_note: axis = CAPABILITY-CLASS, curated on the LEAN-vs-HEAVY lever (same frame as web-research's Fetch anchor). Honest: browser automation is NOT a runtime token-saver by construction; the on-thesis lever is IDLE-DEF — browser-harness adds browser control at 0 MCP tool-defs (skill + a single CDP websocket the agent scripts), vs playwright-MCP's 47 always-on tool-defs for the SAME job. So paw's value = you get the capability without paying 47-tool idle on load-all hosts. SECONDARY ↑quality: the harness is self-healing — it writes missing helpers + per-site domain-skills during execution and reuses them next run, cutting re-derivation loops. NOT over-claimed as a token-saver; curated because it fills the browser-capability gap LEANLY where the obvious alternative is N1-blowing.

token_profile.method: measured = ccusage A/B (portaw bench --ab/--how) on the set's canonical workload, or command-level rtk-gain instrumentation. calculated = tiktoken (cl100k_base proxy) on the verbatim live MCP tool-def JSON the host loads (host tokenizers vary ~10-15%). vendor-claimed = from vendor docs, unverified. estimated = reasoned, not measured. neutral = 0 MCP tool-defs by construction (CLI/hook/skill), idle schema cost provably zero. delta_pct = signed % tokens saved (+ = saved); idle_def_tokens = always-on tool-def cost on load-all hosts (0 on Claude Code, which lazy-loads).

claude-code.sample: 0 MCP servers in set (browser-harness = skill + direct CDP websocket; agent scripts agent-workspace Python).
claude-code.note: Token cost provably zero: no MCP tool-defs loaded on any host. The SKILL.md/install.md content loads ON DEMAND when the browser task is surfaced (progressive disclosure), NOT idle. Contrast playwright-MCP = 47 tool-defs always-on. delta_pct intentionally null: this is a capability, not a token-saver — do NOT fabricate one.

codex.note: 0 MCP idle even on load-all host — this IS the curation lever vs playwright-MCP's 47-tool idle. Harness driven by the agent writing/running Python against one CDP websocket.

gemini.note: same — neutral by construction. (browser-harness setup prompt documents Claude Code + Codex; Gemini support UNVERIFIED — see verified.status.)

non_mcp.purpose: Thin, editable CDP harness: connects the LLM directly to a real Chrome over ONE websocket (nothing between). The agent drives the browser by writing/running Python in agent-workspace, and the harness self-heals — it writes missing helpers + per-site domain-skills during execution and reuses them next run. ~1k lines across 4 core files. 0 MCP tool-defs (skill, not a server).
non_mcp._config_source_note: Install is AGENT-DRIVEN, not a clean package one-liner: the README's documented path is to paste a setup prompt that tells the agent to read install.md and follow it (clones repo, installs the browser_harness package, bootstraps Chrome remote-debugging). Requires the user to enable chrome://inspect/#remote-debugging + Allow the per-attach popup (Chrome 144+). NOT yet install-tested by paw.

install.label: agent-driven setup (paste into Claude Code or Codex)
install.cmd: Set up https://github.com/browser-use/browser-harness for me. Read `install.md` and follow the steps to install browser-harness and connect it to my browser.
install._manual_user_step: Open chrome://inspect/#remote-debugging, tick the checkbox, click Allow on the per-attach popup (Chrome 144+).

optional_env.BH_DOMAIN_SKILLS: 1 to enable agent-workspace/domain-skills (per-site playbooks goto_url surfaces by domain)
optional_env.BROWSER_USE_API_KEY: OPTIONAL — only for Browser Use Cloud (stealth / sub-agents / headless deploy; free tier 3 concurrent). Inject via env/.env ${VAR}, NEVER plaintext (paw security rule). Core local-Chrome use needs NO key.

non_mcp._verified: 2026-06-08 — gh + README browser-use/browser-harness: MIT, Python, 14.5k★. Self-healing CDP harness, ~1k LOC/4 files, skill-based (SKILL.md/install.md), 0 MCP def. Local Chrome use keyless; Browser Use Cloud key optional. Install mechanism = agent-driven setup prompt (NOT a pip/npm one-liner) — recorded honestly; end-to-end install NOT tested.

optional_sources.note: Sibling Claude Skill (TypeScript) that gives the agent a web browser. Lighter-weight CC-oriented alternative to browser-harness. Use when you want a simpler skill on Claude Code specifically; browser-harness is the cross-host (CC+Codex) self-healing anchor. Not bundled — alternative, pick one.

Official Browserbase agent-skills collection for cloud browsers (Stagehand). For when you need MANAGED/cloud browsers (scale, stealth, no local Chrome) — a PAID SaaS path. Keyed. Optional cloud rung, off the keyless-local default; record like web-research's keyed rungs.

rejected.reason: 47-tool MCP = N1-BLOWING (always-on def cost on load-all hosts) for a capability browser-harness delivers at 0 MCP def. This set EXISTS to provide the lean form. Already recorded as rejected in web-research compat for the same reason.

The 97k-star parent FRAMEWORK is a full Python web-agent runtime (its own agent loop) — heavier and runtime-class, not a thin harness the host agent drives. browser-harness is the same authors' THIN CDP harness extracted for exactly the lean-vs-heavy reason. Use the harness, not the framework, inside a coding-agent host.

sets.compat_notes: CURATION VALUE — fills the browser-DRIVING capability gap at 0 MCP tool-defs. Anchor = browser-harness (skill + direct CDP, MIT, cross-host CC+Codex, self-healing). N1-free: 0 MCP defs → stacks with efficiency-starter/secure-agent/context-quality/web-research on any load-all host without touching the ≤2-3 MCP ceiling (this is the entire point vs playwright-MCP's 47). Pairs with web-research: web-research = read/fetch/extract content (no driving); browser-automation = ACT on a page (forms/uploads/flows) when reading isn't enough — non-overlapping. EXCLUSIVE: browser-harness ⊕ dev-browser (pick one browser skill; harness = cross-host+self-healing, dev-browser = simpler CC-only). browserbase-skills = optional CLOUD rung (keyed SaaS) only when local Chrome won't do. SECURITY: agent drives a REAL browser via CDP — treat as high-privilege (can act on logged-in sessions); pair with secure-agent's nah action-guard, and never hardcode the optional cloud key.

verified.status: DRAFT — refs verified (browser-use/browser-harness MIT 14.5k★, dev-browser MIT 6.2k★, browserbase/skills 3.5k★ via gh + README). 0-MCP-def claim verified by construction (skill + CDP, no server). NOT install-tested end-to-end; install is agent-driven (setup prompt → install.md), needs a live run to confirm + capture real steps. Gemini support UNVERIFIED (README documents CC+Codex only). delta_pct intentionally null (capability-class, not a token-saver — neutral idle is the honest profile).
verified._config_block_note: No MCP config to capture (non-MCP skill). Install steps are the agent-driven setup prompt + manual chrome://inspect enable — verify by running once on a real host.

sets.description: Token-lean structured-data answers: query CSV/Parquet/JSON/SQLite with ONE SQL or jq line instead of Read-ing the whole file into context. Pure CLI (DuckDB + jq) — ZERO MCP tool-defs on every host. Token cut by construction: a 50k-row CSV Read costs the whole file; `duckdb -c "SELECT …"` returns only the answer slice.
sets._axis_note: axis = ↓token by construction — the targeted-query-vs-whole-file lever, same frame as web-research's targeted extract but on LOCAL data files where the discovery cost objection does NOT apply: schema discovery is one cheap bounded call (`DESCRIBE SELECT * FROM 'f.csv'` / `jq 'keys'`), not a full-page fetch. Secondary ↑quality: aggregates/joins computed by the engine are EXACT — the agent stops eyeballing rows and hallucinating sums. 0 MCP defs → N1-free, stacks with every other set on any host.

token_profile.method: measured = ccusage A/B (portaw bench --ab/--how) on the set's canonical workload, or command-level rtk-gain instrumentation. calculated = tiktoken (cl100k_base proxy) on the verbatim live MCP tool-def JSON the host loads (host tokenizers vary ~10-15%). vendor-claimed = from vendor docs, unverified. estimated = reasoned, not measured. neutral = 0 MCP tool-defs by construction (CLI/hook/skill), idle schema cost provably zero. delta_pct = signed % tokens saved (+ = saved); idle_def_tokens = always-on tool-def cost on load-all hosts (0 on Claude Code, which lazy-loads).

claude-code.sample: 0 MCP servers (duckdb + jq = CLI on demand). Runtime A/B RAN 2026-06-12 (bench/_dataquery_ab.py, deterministic tiktoken cl100k).
claude-code.note: Idle provably zero. Runtime delta CALCULATED 2026-06-12: 2.02MB / 50k-row CSV = 1,099,015 tok to Read whole-file vs 477 tok for duckdb schema-discovery (72) + 3 query answers (count-by-region 98 / filter-top5 195 / revenue-by-region 112) = +99.96%. Mechanism is structural: query output bounded by the query (LIMIT/aggregate), whole-file Read bounded by the file. The 2MB case is dramatic because a 50k-row Read is enormous (also exceeds host Read limits → many calls); the bounded-vs-unbounded truth holds at any size.

codex.note: CLI invoked on demand; no startup schema cost even on load-all host.

non_mcp._version_note: v1.5.2 = latest stable 2026-04-13; v1.4.x = LTS line (supported 1 year). Either is fine; pin at install.
non_mcp.purpose: In-process SQL engine that queries CSV/Parquet/JSON/SQLite FILES directly (`FROM 'data.csv'`, `sqlite_scan(...)`, globs over partitioned parquet) — no import step, no server. The agent answers filter/aggregate/join questions in one bounded call instead of paging a whole data file through context.

install.label: install duckdb CLI
install._alt_sh: curl https://install.duckdb.org | sh (universal, but shell-pipe = print-only)

non_mcp.usage_hint: duckdb -c "SELECT col, count(*) FROM 'data.csv' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 10"   |   schema first: duckdb -c "DESCRIBE SELECT * FROM 'data.csv'"   |   duckdb -json -c "FROM 'logs/*.parquet' LIMIT 5"   |   duckdb -c "FROM sqlite_scan('app.db','users') LIMIT 5"

non_mcp.purpose: Command-line JSON processor: pull exactly the fields/paths needed from a large JSON file or API response instead of Read-ing the whole document. Composable with curl/gh pipelines the agent already runs.

install.label: install jq
install._alt_brew: Linux without Linuxbrew: sudo apt/dnf/pacman install jq (distro-dependent, needs root → run manually)

non_mcp.usage_hint: jq '.items[] | {id, name}' big.json   |   jq -r '.[].url' resp.json   |   shape first: jq 'keys' obj.json / jq 'length' arr.json

optional_sources.note: Same slicing lever for YAML/XML/TOML (jq syntax). Optional rung — add when the workload is config-file-heavy (k8s/CI manifests). winget install MikeFarah.yq / brew install yq.

rejected.reason: MCP server wrapping a LOCAL CLI = pure idle-def cost for zero capability gain — `duckdb -c` is already one bounded call with no session state worth holding. Same lean-vs-heavy verdict as playwright-MCP vs browser-harness.
rejected.axis: idle-def blow for no capability delta

rejected.reason: Works but costs a multi-line script + interpreter session per question; duckdb answers the same filter/aggregate question in one flag-bounded shell call. Not rejected from the agent's toolbox — just not the curated lever.
rejected.axis: more tokens per answer, same result

sets.compat_notes: CURATION VALUE — the targeted-query lever for LOCAL structured data, 0 MCP defs. Non-overlapping with neighbours: codegraph = code structure, data-query = data FILES; web-research = remote pages, data-query = local files; doc-extract = RENDER binary office docs to readable md, data-query = QUERY-shaped questions (xlsx overlap rule: 'what does the sheet say' → markitdown render; 'sum/filter/join the rows' → duckdb). rtk: no hook collision (rtk rewrites its known command list; duckdb/jq pass through, and their output is already bounded by the query — nothing to compress). N1: 0 MCP → stacks with everything. Both tools keyless, offline, single binary.

verified.status: verified — INSTALL-TESTED + runtime A/B done 2026-06-12 on this Windows box. `winget install DuckDB.cli` -> duckdb v1.5.3 (note: web said v1.5.2; v1.5.3 was current at install — newer, fine), `winget install jqlang.jq` -> jq-1.8.1. `portaw verify data-query` gate PASS (both health_binary which-probes ok). Runtime delta CALCULATED via bench/_dataquery_ab.py = +99.96% (whole-file Read 1.099M tok vs duckdb schema+3-queries 477 tok on a 2.02MB/50k-row CSV). WINDOWS NOTE: winget PATH change is not visible to an already-spawned shell — merge Machine+User PATH (or restart shell) before verify.
verified.config_block_confirmed: n/a — no MCP config; install commands quoted from official docs (install.duckdb.org, jqlang download page)
verified.token_profile_benchmarked: CC runtime delta CALCULATED 2026-06-12 (+99.96%, deterministic tiktoken, bench/_dataquery_ab.py). jq lane not separately benched (same bounded-slice mechanism).

sets.description: Read the unreadable: ONE CLI call (Microsoft MarkItDown) converts docx/xlsx/pptx — formats host Read tools cannot open at all — plus PDF/HTML/images to clean structure-preserving markdown. The agent then greps/slices the markdown section it needs instead of failing on a binary blob. 0 MCP tool-defs (CLI anchor).
sets._axis_note: axis = ↑context-quality FIRST (binary office files are invisible to the agent without this — a capability gap, like browser-automation), ↓token second (markdown that preserves headings/tables lets the agent slice the relevant section; honest: unmeasured). HONEST CC NOTE: Claude Code's native Read already handles PDF and images — on CC the real gap is docx/xlsx/pptx ONLY; the PDF/image rungs matter on hosts WITHOUT native PDF read (Codex/Gemini CLI). delta_pct intentionally null — capability-class, do NOT fabricate a saving.

token_profile.method: measured = ccusage A/B (portaw bench --ab/--how) on the set's canonical workload, or command-level rtk-gain instrumentation. calculated = tiktoken (cl100k_base proxy) on the verbatim live MCP tool-def JSON the host loads (host tokenizers vary ~10-15%). vendor-claimed = from vendor docs, unverified. estimated = reasoned, not measured. neutral = 0 MCP tool-defs by construction (CLI/hook/skill), idle schema cost provably zero. delta_pct = signed % tokens saved (+ = saved); idle_def_tokens = always-on tool-def cost on load-all hosts (0 on Claude Code, which lazy-loads).

claude-code.note: Idle provably zero. Capability-primary: on CC the win is docx/xlsx/pptx (Read opens PDF natively). delta_pct null by honesty — converting then slicing is usually cheaper than nothing-at-all, which is the real baseline for these formats.

codex.note: CLI on demand; PDF rung ALSO matters here (no native PDF read).

gemini.note: same — neutral by construction; PDF rung matters.

non_mcp.purpose: Official Microsoft converter: docx/xlsx/pptx/pdf/html/images (15+ formats) → markdown that PRESERVES structure (headings, lists, tables, links) — built explicitly for LLM pipelines. One call makes a binary office file greppable/sliceable text.

install.label: install markitdown (pip, scoped extras — NOT [all])
install._windows_note: on this box: py -m pip install "markitdown[docx,xlsx,pptx,pdf]"
install._all_trap: AVOID `markitdown[all]` — it pins youtube-transcript-api~=1.0.0 (gone from PyPI) -> unsatisfiable -> pip SILENTLY backtracks markitdown to ancient 0.0.2 (microsoft/markitdown #1809/#1704). Scoped extras (the office/pdf converters this set needs) pin a current 0.1.x cleanly. Add the youtube/audio extras only if that capability is actually wanted.

non_mcp.usage_hint: markitdown report.docx -o report.md   |   markitdown sheet.xlsx   |   markitdown deck.pptx   |   then grep/slice the md instead of re-converting

optional_sources.note: OFFICIAL MCP form — exactly 1 tool `convert_to_markdown(uri)` (http/https/file/data URIs) = leanest-possible MCP shape (Fetch-class; est. ~250-300 tok idle, NOT tokenized yet). Default stays the CLI (0 idle); take this rung only where shell access is awkward. Local-trusted-agent use only per its README (binds localhost).

Universal document converter, BOTH directions (md → docx/pdf output too — markitdown is read-only md). Heavyweight install; add only when the workload needs to PRODUCE office/pdf deliverables, not just read them.

rejected.tool: community markitdown MCP wrappers (trsdn/markitdown-mcp, KorigamiK, npx wrappers)
rejected.reason: Official CLI + official 1-tool MCP already cover both shapes; third-party wrappers add supply-chain surface for zero capability delta (paw security: smallest reviewed surface).

sets.compat_notes: CURATION VALUE — fills the binary-document blind spot at 0 MCP tool-defs. Non-overlapping with neighbours: web-research = remote web pages; doc-extract = LOCAL binary documents; data-query = query-shaped questions (xlsx rule: 'what does the sheet say' → markitdown render; 'sum/filter the rows' → duckdb); context-quality = library docs. N1: 0 MCP → stacks with everything. Anchor keyless/offline after pip install. SECURITY: converting UNTRUSTED documents executes parser code over hostile input — keep markitdown current (pin-at-install, bump on advisories); pairs with secure-agent.

verified.status: verified — INSTALL-TESTED + capability probe done 2026-06-12 on this Windows box. `py -m pip install "markitdown[all]"` -> `portaw verify doc-extract` gate PASS. Capability probe (bench/_docextract_probe.py): generated a docx (h1/h2 headings + 3-col table) and an xlsx (named sheet + 10 rows), converted both via markitdown -> markdown PRESERVED structure = `# Quarterly Report` + `## Summary`/`## Regional Breakdown`, table rendered as GFM `| Region | Revenue | Growth |`, xlsx sheet name + all columns + all 10 rows intact. VERDICT structure-preserving = True. ⚠️ VERSION FINDING (root cause CORRECTED 2026-06-12): pip installed markitdown **0.0.2**, NOT v0.1.6. Cause is NOT a Python-3.14 wheel gap (markitdown 0.1.6 is pure `py3-none-any.whl`, installs fine on 3.14 with --no-deps). REAL cause = the `[all]` extra pins `youtube-transcript-api~=1.0.0`, which is no longer on PyPI (only 0.6.x then 1.2.3+), so `markitdown[all]==0.1.x` is unsatisfiable and pip SILENTLY backtracks markitdown down to 0.0.2. Known upstream: microsoft/markitdown #1809 (closed COMPLETED), #1704, #103 — NOT re-filed (duplicate). WORKAROUND (verified): install only needed extras, not `[all]` — `py -m pip install "markitdown[docx,xlsx,pptx,pdf]==0.1.6"` resolves 0.1.6 correctly. 0.0.2 still converts docx/xlsx fine for this set's purpose. delta_pct stays null (capability-class). TODO: switch the install step to the scoped-extras form to pin 0.1.x.
verified.config_block_confirmed: n/a for CLI anchor; markitdown-mcp config shape from official package README (command markitdown-mcp), not host-captured
